Johnson University Hospital.

It's Friday, which is my normal operative day

Good morning Dr. Lee
How are you?

Three of the cases are minimally invasive 
cases and one case is a coronary artery bypass.

So COVID has really impacted how

Some of the contact made 
after surgery is by phone.
Morning.
Patients in the room.

Once we hook up the patient to the monitors we do 
what's called a timeout, which is a safety check.
All right, time out.

The timeout process that we do in the operating 
room comes from the World Health Organization

and it is a means to be very careful and 
to ensure that we don't make mistakes

This woman is 62 years old she has what's called 
aortic stenosis. The valve at the top of the heart

called the aortic valve has become very tight over 
time and as a consequence, her heart has to work

extra hard to try to push blood through that 
tight valve. When all the blood can't go forward

it starts backing up and it backs up into the 
lungs and causes shortness of breath. Can also

cause chest pain because the heart's working 
extra hard. It can also cause lightheadedness

because not enough blood is actually escaping 
in the heart and going up into the brain.

So what we're doing today is what's called a 
minimally invasive aortic valve replacement.

So everything else in the heart is in 
good working order she'll have about

a three-inch incision on her right 
chest where we go between the ribs

and through there we can get in 
there to then replace the valve.
Okay you can ventilate both lungs.
It's fairly straightforward case everything went

well as expected. So I'm going 
to just call the family member.
Hello.
Good morning it's Dr Lee calling from Robert Wood.
Good morning Dr. Lee.

We're all done with surgery. Everything 
went well. She got the valve replaced

through the small incision on 
her right side. She's doing okay.
This is great news. Thank you so very much.
All right we'll see you a little later, okay.
Okay, have a good day.

it's everyone in the ICU, it's everyone

attention to detail - all of that is 
incredibly important to the success.
It is now nine o'clock, we're in the second room.

We are a very high volume center

that show that the more you do

if this is left alone and not fixed

She'll have an incision right down the middle

We don't touch the blockages, we leave the
